channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Do You Really Need WebSockets?
item.description: Every time a feature had the word "real-time" in it, my brain would jump straight to WebSockets. It felt like the right, serious, production-grade choice. Turns out, it's often overkill. And...

item.title: How to use AI to identify and fix security vulnerabilities in your codebase
item.description: Meta: Understand the common code-based security vulnerabilities, from SQL injection to XSS, and how AI simplifies the detection and resolution of these security vulnerabilities for improved code...

item.title: The Pope’s Warnings About AI Were AI-Generated, a Detection Tool Claims
item.description: Pangram Labs’ updated Chrome extension puts warning labels on AI slop as you scroll your social feeds.

item.title: Anker made its own chip to bring AI to all its products
item.description: Anker has announced its own custom silicon that the company says will bring local AI to audio devices, mobile accessories, and IoT devices. The Thus processor is the world's first neural-net...

item.title: How I built a QR business card generator with Next.js
item.description: How I built a QR business card generator with Next.js Most small businesses still rely on printed visiting cards that get outdated quickly and are hard to track or update. I wanted to solve a...

item.title: Building The World Now: six months of stack notes
item.description: About six months ago I started The World Now as a side project, an attempt to build a news site where the pipeline is mostly automated but the editorial judgment still feels like a human made the...

item.title: Why McDonald’s AI Started Coding: A Wake-Up Call for Chatbot Security
item.description: Imagine you’re hungry, you open the McDonald’s app to complain about a missing Big Mac, and instead of a refund, the chatbot starts writing Python scripts for you. Sounds like a developer's dream?...

item.title: Why CAPTCHA and Traditional Verification Methods Are Failing
item.description: Our defenses against automated threats that we have constructed to ensure that human-facing systems do not receive them are being systematically bypassed, not by some exotic exploit, but with the...

item.title: How I Started Learning Kotlin by Building a Real Android App
item.description: For a long time, Kotlin was one of those technologies I kept meaning to learn “properly.” You probably know the feeling. You read a few examples. You understand the syntax. You save a few...

item.title: Anthropic’s most dangerous AI model just fell into the wrong hands
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos AI model, a powerful cybersecurity tool that the company said could be dangerous in the wrong hands, has been accessed by a "small group of unauthorized users," Bloomberg...

item.title: Bringing Java Closer to Education: A Community-Driven Initiative
item.description: Story Horse? Java has long been one of the most powerful, stable, and widely-used programming languages in the world. Yet when it comes to education, especially for beginners, schools, and coding...

item.title: Build a 24/7 Facebook Messenger AI Chatbot With n8n (Full Setup — No Code)
item.description: What this builds A Facebook Messenger chatbot that responds to every incoming message automatically, 24/7, using an AI agent connected to your Facebook Business Page via n8n. Customers message...

item.title: Fonctionnement d’une blockchain - Étape 3/8 : Sélection des transactions (frais & priorité)
item.description: Ici, les transactions (ex : “Alice envoie 10 tokens à Bob”) ont été créées, signées et vérifiées. Elles attendent maintenant dans la mempool (la file d’attente). Mais… qui décide quand et comment...

item.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a...

item.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated...

item.title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
item.description: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

item.title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
item.description: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

item.title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
item.description: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the...

item.title: SpaceX cuts a deal to maybe buy Cursor for $60 billion
item.description: With an IPO looming for Elon Musk's SpaceX / xAI / X combo platter of companies, SpaceX has announced an odd arrangement to either acquire the automated programming platform Cursor for $60 billion...

item.title: Roundtables: Unveiling The 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Watch a special edition of Roundtables simulcast live from EmTech AI, MIT Technology Review’s signature conference for AI leadership. Subscribers got an...

item.title: We translated the Palantir manifesto for actual human beings
item.description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp is a man in charge of one of the most important and frightening companies in the world. Karp's new book, cowritten with Nicholas Zamiska, is called The Technological...

item.title: The new word in home construction could be “plastics”
item.description: Single-use plastics are a persistent source of environmental pollution, and the need to house a growing global population puts increasing pressure on resources such as timber. MIT engineers have...

item.title: A natural protein may protect the GI tract from infection
item.description: Embedded in the body’s mucosal surfaces, proteins called lectins bind to sugars found on cell surfaces. A team led by MIT chemistry professor Laura Kiessling has found that one such protein,...

item.title: This tool could show how consciousness works
item.description: How does the physical matter in our brains translate into thoughts, sensations, and emotions? It’s hard to explore that question without neurosurgery. But in a recent paper, MIT philosopher...

item.title: Early life may have breathed oxygen earlier than believed
item.description: Around 2.3 billion years ago, a pivotal period known as the Great Oxidation Event set the evolutionary course for oxygen-breathing life on Earth. But MIT geobiologists and colleagues have found...

item.title: Analog computing from waste heat
item.description: Heat generated by electronic devices is usually a problem, but a team led by Giuseppe Romano, a research scientist at MIT’s Institute for Soldier Nanotechnologies, has found a way to use it for...

item.title: ISS astronauts are getting new laptops
item.description: Even astronauts need to level up their laptops once in a while - including the crew of Expedition 74 on board the ISS, which NASA announced last week is in the process of some computer upgrades....

item.title: Tim Cook was an innovator — just not the Jobs kind
item.description: Under Steve Jobs, Apple released the groundbreaking products that defined the company. But the company wouldn't be what it is today without Tim Cook's reign of ruthless efficiency. Jobs' legacy...

item.title: AI backlash is coming for elections
item.description: Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI...

item.title: OpenAI’s updated image generator can now pull information from the web
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out the latest version of its AI-powered image generator with new "thinking capabilities," allowing it to search the web to help it create multiple images from a single prompt....
